questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph A small block of mass $m$, can move without friction on the outside of a fixed vertical circular track of radius $R$. The block is attached to a spring of natural length $R / 2$ and spring constant $k$. The other end of spring is connected to a point at height $R / 2$ directly above the centre of track.
Question If the block is released from rest when the spring is in horizontal state (see figure) then at that moment:
questions.solution:
$F_t=m g \sin \theta-k x \cos \theta ; \theta=60^{\circ}$
$a_t=g \sin 60^{\circ}-\frac{k x}{m} \cos 60^{\circ}=\frac{\sqrt{3} g}{2}-\frac{k R}{4 m}(\sqrt{3}-1)$
$a_c=v^2 / r=0$ released from rest
S.E. $=\frac{1}{2} k R^2\left(\sin \theta-\frac{1}{2}\right)^2=\frac{1}{2} k R^2\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{2}\right)^2$
$=\frac{k R^2}{8}(\sqrt{3}-1)^2$
$\frac{1}{2} m u^2=\frac{1}{2} k(R)^2$
$u=R \sqrt{\frac{k}{m}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph The cross-section of a fixed cylinder (not allowed to rotate and translate) with horizontal axis is as shown. One end of a light inelastic string is fixed at top of cylinder of radius R and a small block of mass m is tied to the other end of string. Initially the block is at rest with the portion of string not in contact with cylinder being vertical and having length $L$ as shown. At the lowest position the block is given initial horizontal velocity $\mathrm{u}=\sqrt{2 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~L}}$ and the block moves in vertical plane. When the block reaches the highest point of its trajectory, the length of string not in contact with cylinder is $L+\frac{R \pi}{3}$. (where g is acceleration due to gravity).
Question The distance between block and centre of cylinder when block is at highest position will be:
questions.solution:
Since $\mathrm{u}=\sqrt{2 \mathrm{gL}}$, the highest point to which the block shall reach is $\frac{\mathrm{u}^2}{2 \mathrm{~g}}=\mathrm{L}$ distance above its initial position. Hence at highest point, the thread has rotated by $\frac{\pi}{3}=60^{\circ}$ and the block is at same horizontal level as centre of cylinder as shown.
$\begin{gathered} x=\frac{R}{\sin 30}=2 R \\ T=m g \cos 60^{\circ}=\frac{m g}{2} \end{gathered}$ tangential acceleration $\mathrm{a_t}=\mathrm{g} \sin 60^{\circ}=\frac{\sqrt{3} \mathrm{~g}}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A light rod of length $L$, is hanging from the vertical smooth wall of a vehicle moving with acceleration $\sqrt{3} \mathrm{~g}$ having a small mass attached at it's one end is free to rotate about an axis passing through the other end. The minimum velocity given to the mass at it's equilibrium position so that it can complete vertical circular motion is
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the position shown, the spring is at its natural length. The block of mass $m$ is given a velocity $v_0$ towards the vertical support at $t=0$. The coefficient of friction between the block and the surface is given by: $\mu=\alpha \mathrm{x}$, where $\alpha$ is a positive constant and x is the position of the block from its starting position. The block comes to rest for the first time at $\mathrm{x}=$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & -v \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{(k+\alpha m g) x}{m} \\ & \Rightarrow-\int_{v_0}^0 v d v=\left(\frac{k+\alpha m g}{m}\right) \int_0^{x_0} x d x \\ & \Rightarrow x_0=v_0 \sqrt{\frac{m}{(k+\alpha m g)}}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
